🚁 SAHANA SYSTEM – ANTI-DRONE OPPORTUNITY (QUICK ANALYSIS)

🧭 BUSINESS POSITIONING
• Sahana System Limited focusing on Counter-UAS (anti-drone systems)
• Operates in niche defense electronics + AI-driven surveillance
• Positioned in high-growth, emerging warfare tech segment

⚔️ INDUSTRY TAILWIND
• Rising drone usage in modern warfare → demand for detection + jamming + EW systems
• Anti-drone becoming mandatory defense layer globally
• Strong push from Indian government for indigenisation (Make in India)

🧪 TECHNOLOGY EDGE
• Capabilities in:
• RF (Radio Frequency detection)
• Radar systems
• AI-based tracking & threat identification
• Plays in integrated anti-drone solutions (not just hardware)

📦 ORDER FLOW & VALIDATION
• ₹24.69 Cr order from Central Electronics Limited
• Collaboration with Bharat Electronics Limited
• Association with BEL = strong credibility + future pipeline potential

📈 GROWTH DRIVERS
• Defense budget allocation increasing in India
• Border security + critical infrastructure protection demand
• Export opportunity in friendly nations
• Anti-drone shifting from optional → essential

⚠️ KEY MONITORABLES
• Order inflow consistency (lumpy defense business)
• Execution capability (small company vs large contracts)
• Competition from established players (BEL, DRDO ecosystem)
• Scalability beyond initial contracts

🏆 INVESTMENT VIEW (STRAIGHT TAKE)
• High potential but early-stage + execution dependent story
• Works best as:
• High-risk, high-reward defense niche play
• Not yet a proven large-scale execution company

🧠 ONE LINE TAKE
• “Sahana = Early entrant in anti-drone theme, but real wealth depends on scaling orders, not just opportunity visibility.”

- Max: Best 3Y phase.  
- Min: Downside pain points.

- Short-term 3Y outcomes vary wildly.  
- Demands long horizons + realistic expectations.

**🇮🇳 INDIA SEMICONDUCTOR SECTOR – QUICK INVESTMENT SNAPSHOT**

**🧭 OVERALL VIEW**
• Early-stage capex-led upcycle
• Strong tailwinds from AI + electronics + policy
• Still import dependent + execution risk
• Strategy = selective play, not broad betting

**📊 DEMAND DRIVERS**
**Short Term (2–3 yrs)**
• Smartphones + electronics manufacturing boom
• 5G rollout + data centre expansion (AI infra push)
• China+1 shift (Apple, global OEMs)

**Long Term (5–10 yrs)**
• AI devices → higher chip content per device
• EVs + auto electronics + defence demand
• Cloud + AI ecosystem expansion

**🏭 SUPPLY SIDE POSITIONING**
• Strong in design (20% global talent)
• Emerging in ATMP/OSAT (Micron, Tata, CG Power JV)
• Weak in fabs & materials (import heavy)
• Focus = mature nodes (28–65nm), not advanced nodes

**🏗 KEY SEGMENT OPPORTUNITIES**
**Fabs / OSAT (Manufacturing)**
• High capex + high operating leverage
• Beneficiaries: Tata Electronics, Micron, CG Power JV
• Risk: execution + utilisation

**Fabless / IP (Best ROI Segment)**
• Asset-light, high margin
• Focus areas: edge-AI, EV chips, RF, analog
• Strong startup + design ecosystem

**Materials / Equipment (Import Substitution)**
• Huge opportunity (90% import dependency)
• Early stage → long runway
• Policy support via ECMS

**💰 MARGIN DRIVERS**
• Govt subsidy (up to 50% capex support)
• Operating leverage (utilisation ramp-up)
• Product mix (IP > manufacturing margins)
• Raw material import dependency (key risk)

**⚡️ KEY STRUCTURAL TRENDS**
• Shift to compute-driven AI economy
• Supply chain becoming security-driven (not cost-driven)
• India evolving → design → packaging → partial manufacturing
• Policy shift → ecosystem + IP creation focus

**🏛 POLICY SUPPORT**
• ₹76,000 Cr ISM + ₹40,000 Cr ECMS
• 50% subsidy for fabs
• Design incentives (DLI: 4–6% sales support)
• Tax benefits + talent push (85,000 VLSI engineers/year target)

**🌍 GLOBAL CONTEXT**
• Taiwan dominates advanced chips
• US leads design/IP
• China controls materials
• India = design + backend hub emerging

**📈 GROWTH OUTLOOK (2–3 YEARS)**
• Market growth: ~15–18% CAGR
• Early gains from ATMP/OSAT ramp-up
• Fabless design companies gaining traction
• Capex pipeline strong ($15B+ committed)

**⚠️ KEY RISKS**
• Execution delays (infra, fabs)
• High import dependency
• Low R&D spend (~0.6% GDP)
• Talent shortage / brain drain
• Future overcapacity risk

**🎯 INVESTMENT STRATEGY (MOST IMPORTANT)**
**Best Bet (High Conviction)**
• Fabless / IP companies (high margin, scalable)

**Second Layer**
• OSAT / ATMP players (operating leverage play)

**Early Stage Bets**
• Materials & equipment (import substitution theme)

**Avoid / Be Cautious**
• Low value assembly players
• Overhyped fab stories (long gestation, low ROCE risk)

**🧠 ONE LINE TAKE**
• India semiconductor = Design + Packaging story now, Manufacturing story later 🚀

**OIL SHOCK: NIFTY 15-20% RISK**  

**Thesis**  
- $25-30/bbl spike hits GDP 1-2pp, EPS 12%→5-7%, P/E 20x→17-18x.  
- Cyclical; digital/manuf/energy drives rebound.  

**Drivers**  
- GDP 6.5-6.7% to #3 by FY31.  
- Datacenters #2 APAC; EVs/H2/nuclear cut oil use.  
- Exports/domestic buffer.  

**Sectors**  
- **Severe neg**: OMCs (under-recoveries), aviation (ATF).  
- **Neg**: Financials (credit 7-8%), auto/chemicals/paints.  
- **Mild neg**: FMCG.  
- **Pos**: Upstream O&G, pharma, defence, digital infra.  

**Commentary**  
- $10/bbl = 0.2pp GDP drag; RBI delay 2-4Q.  

**Financials**  
- Crude bill $130-140 Bn; Nifty stress P/E 17-18x.  

**Valuations**  
- Normal 20-22x; post-bottom 90%+ gains.  

**Risks**  
- Crude persistence, rupee inflation, fiscal, China/Russia.  

**Triggers**  
- **Near**: Oil stabilise, rupee/RBI/yields.  
- **Med**: Credit revival, structural wins.  

**Bull/Bear**  
- **Bear**: 15-20% drop.  
- **Bull**: Quick peak →90% rally.  

**Key Takeaway:** Buy upstream/digital on weakness; await oil peak.

- GDP 7%/7.6%/6.8%; upstream/refiners gain short-term.

- **Financials**: Tier-2/3 banks/NBFCs cheap P/B.  
- **Consumption**: FMCG/QSR/retail/paints rich fwd P/E – reduce.  
- **Oil&Gas**: Upstream/refiners up; OMCs down.

- Nifty EPS: FY26E ₹1,072, FY27E ₹1,246, FY28E ₹1,432.

- **Near**: Conflict evolution, govt fuel stance, corp gas commentary.

text:
Management expects domestic demand (across Asia) to remain robust & outpace modest growth expectation of 2-4% CAGR globally, broadly driven by renewable & electrification, infra spending, packaging and auto/EV adoption
Impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is largely limited to rising energy (coal) costs
Management noted that 75% of energy is fulfilled via coal linkages & rest via e-auction; therefore, rise in coal e-auction prices can increase its energy costs
HNDL’s cost mix stands as 40% coal, 35% alumina and 25% others (incl. caustic soda and petcoke/CP coke, etc.)
As a mitigation strategy, HNDL targets to be 100% captive by FY33 via three captive mines (Chakla – 1HFY27, Bandha - FY27 and Meenakshi - FY29), resulting in ~USD200/t in direct cost savings
Management plans to expand value-added product offerings (both copper and aluminum), with an aim of cushioning the margins and achieving higher downstream EBITDA in India over the medium term
HNDL highlighted that Aditya FRP and battery enclosure facility is currently ramping up, while it is expecting the commissioning of the IGT, battery foil and AC fin.

*Jefferies on JSW Infra*
Buy, TP Rs 360
JSW Infra management in recent interactions reiterated its target to double EBITDA over FY26E-28E, led by capacity additions which are on track.
Logistics (5% of FY26E EBITDA) growth plans are ahead of schedule.
Geopolitical tensions are a near-term headwind
Raise FY28E EBITDA by 10%, to reflect the optimism on project progress and estimate 29% FY26E-30E EBITDA CAGR, led by 19% volume CAGR, where group expansion plans lend utilisation visibility.

*Jefferies on Power Demand*
Feb 2026 power demand rose just 1% YoY on a base of 2% YoY growth in Feb 2025.
Apr 2025-Feb 2026 demand is up only 1% YoY largely driven by above average monsoons.
Our 2% FY26E power demand growth assumption needs 14% YoY rise in March 2026.
Generation is up just 2% YoY in the first 12 days of March but with rising temperatures should recover ahead.
JSW Energy & NTPC remain top picks

*Jefferies on Finolex Cables*
Buy, TP Rs 1025
Management meet takeaways
FNXC took 5 price hikes, total +12% in Dec25-Q, & 2 hikes in Jan-Feb26 (+5%) to pass-on copper surge
Electrical Wires saw strong volumes at +28%YoY in Dec-Q (restocking).
But, Mar-Q has a high base of LY.
Data centers can benefit Power Cables demand.
BharatNet orders can drive OFC volume
Est FY25-28e EPS CAGR at +17%, but prolonged ME conflict can impact end-user demand, polymer supply.

*JPM On Cement sector*
Dealer checks highlight
(1) some price hikes, mostly in the non-trade segment
(2) weaker-than-expected demand given March is usually a very strong month for cement volumes.
Given that prices haven’t seen much of a hike & demand remains weak, there is a risk that companies could miss 4Q (& overall FY25) Street expectations
Maintain cautious stance on sector with UTCEM only OW

*GS on Cement Sector*
Incorporate initial impact of current energy shortage & prices for cement cos
MTM pet-coke and coal prices (lag impact due to 60-75 days inventory), expected lower demand in Q4 and Q1 driven by shortages in packing bags and in FY27 by infrastructure related demand for cement
Offset this partly through price increases based on company commentary
If energy costs remain elevated for a longer period, we may see impact of lower Infra spending
Stay Buy on Ultratech, Neutral on Shree, Dalmia, Ambuja, JSW & Sell on ACC

*Macquarie on Paints*
Berger Paints and Kansai Nerolac announce 2-3% price hikes
Price hikes are effective 25-March with BRGR indicating that a phase-two price increase is likely in April
Margin headwinds a near-term concern
Asian Paints – O-P, TP Rs 3100
Berger Paints – U-P, TP Rs 410
Kansai – Neutral, TP Rs 220

text:
**HSBC Tata Motors PV Hold TP Rs 340 (from 400)**

**JLR Pressures**  
West Asia exposure + RM inflation.  
Recovery outlook challenging.

**India Tailwinds**  
Sierra launch strong.  
Harrier petrol demand incremental.

**Key Takeaway**  
JLR risks cap upside despite India momentum

India's BESS Boom: Capacity Surge, Execution Risk  

Market Momentum:  
- Sharp rise in standalone BESS tendering over past year.  
- Solar + BESS now cheapest dispatchable power, extending beyond solar hours.  
- BESS shifting from optional to essential in power stack.  

Capacity Pipeline:  
- ~31 GWh (~15 GW) standalone BESS by FY28 (awarded + ongoing tenders).  
- Phasing: FY26: ~2 GWh; FY27: ~10 GWh; FY28: ~19 GWh.  
- Assumes ~1 year from award to commissioning.  

Key Players:  
- State utilities driving bulk of near-term awards.  
- NTPC strategic participant.  
- Private/non-utility players winning majority capacity.  

Tariff Trends:  
- Early tenders: ₹1.8L+ per MWh/month.  
- Recent wins: ₹70k-₹90k per MWh/month.  
- Driven by falling battery costs, intense competition, aggressive entry bids.  

Structural Shift:  
- Optimizes transmission, balances renewables.  
- Reduces need for pumped storage, incremental thermal capacity.  
- Focuses on system efficiency, not capacity inflation.  

Execution Risks:  
- Thin margins from aggressive tariffs.  
- Battery sourcing volatility, financing stress.  
- Untested EPC/O&M at scale, potential commissioning delays.  

Investor Red Flags:  
- Low entry barriers attracting underprepared players.  
- Non-utilities dominating wins, limited operating history.  
- Expect attrition, consolidation, slippages.  

Key Takeaway:  
BESS capacity is locked in, but execution discipline separates survivors from casualties.

text:
MOSL on Hindalco

Buy, TP Rs 1110

Management meet takeaways,

Management expects domestic demand (across Asia) to remain robust & outpace modest growth expectation of 2-4% CAGR globally, broadly driven by renewable & electrification, infra spending, packaging and auto/EV adoption

Impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is largely limited to rising energy (coal) costs

Management noted that 75% of energy is fulfilled via coal linkages & rest via e-auction; therefore, rise in coal e-auction prices can increase its energy costs

HNDL’s cost mix stands as 40% coal, 35% alumina and 25% others (incl. caustic soda and petcoke/CP coke, etc.)

As a mitigation strategy, HNDL targets to be 100% captive by FY33 via three captive mines (Chakla – 1HFY27, Bandha - FY27 and Meenakshi - FY29), resulting in ~USD200/t in direct cost savings

Management plans to expand value-added product offerings (both copper and aluminum), with an aim of cushioning the margins and achieving higher downstream EBITDA in India over the medium term

HNDL highlighted that Aditya FRP and battery enclosure facility is currently ramping up, while it is expecting the commissioning of the IGT, battery foil and AC fin.

text:
Mankind Pharma Plans Early Semaglutide Launch in India

Mankind Pharma aims to be among the first companies to launch semaglutide in India after the drug’s patent expires on March 20.

India will be the primary launch market, while international markets will be explored later.

The company plans to launch the drug independently, with no co-marketing or partnership arrangements.

Semaglutide is widely used for diabetes and weight management, and demand is rising amid growing obesity and metabolic health concerns.

Mankind plans to price the drug carefully to balance affordability and long-term sustainability, continuing its strategy of introducing affordable medicines.

The company expects strong market expansion, as the patent expiry could allow around 50 generic brands to enter the Indian market.

Beyond diabetes, semaglutide has potential applications across cardiology, orthopaedics, and gynaecology, expanding its therapeutic scope.

The Shrinking MOAT — A Risk Investors Are Underpricing

The Core Observation
- Sanjoy Bhattacharya: *"Period of competitive advantage has shortened dramatically worldwide"*
- MOATs that once lasted decades are now lasting years
- Rate of disruption is accelerating faster than ever

The Valuation Trap
- Assigning high terminal value to MOATs = hidden risk
- Markets price in perpetual competitive advantage
- When MOAT breaks → sudden & sharp de-rating event
- Multiples built on MOATs can become irrational until they collapse

Why MOATs Are Breaking Faster
- Democratisation of capital — funding is more accessible
- Technology — levels the playing field rapidly
- AI — disrupts conventional business models at scale
- Lower barriers — new entrants can replicate faster than before

30 Years Ago vs Today
- Earlier: MOATs were extremely difficult to break
- Today: Competitive advantages are far more vulnerable
- Conventional business models now face real terminal value risk

The Nuanced View
- Some MOATs do deserve premium multiples
- But assuming perpetuity is where the danger lies
- Investors must ask: *"Is this MOAT still intact — or just priced as if it is?"*

Key Takeaway
- Price in MOAT durability — not just MOAT existence
- A broken MOAT + high multiple = significant wealth destruction
- In today's world, re-examine every terminal value assumption

West Asia Tensions Weigh on India’s Gems & Jewellery Exports  

Export Pressure Mounts  
- India’s gems and jewellery exports have declined ~20%, impacted by geopolitical instability in West Asia, per GJEPC.  
- If disruptions persist, exports worth ~$1.2 billion could be hit in the next three months.  

Regional Dependence  
- About 20% of India’s jewellery exports are directed to West Asia, exposing the sector to regional volatility.  
- The UAE and GCC together account for ~$8.3 billion in exports, with Dubai serving as a major hub for polished diamond trading.  

Trade Linkages at Risk  
- Gold imports to the UAE (~200 tonnes annually) could face hurdles, given the India–UAE CEPA dependencies in the supply chain.  

Broader Context  
- From Apr 2025–Feb 2026, exports were flat at $25.93 billion, nearly unchanged from $25.92 billion YoY, reflecting a sluggish global demand cycle.  
- The industry is also navigating global trade uncertainties and potential US tariff pressures, further straining export momentum.  

Key Takeaway:  
Persistent West Asia tensions and global trade headwinds could delay recovery for India’s jewellery export sector, underscoring the need for market diversification and supply-chain resilience.

**Home Decor Sector — Raw Material Shock | Nuvama Sector Update Mar-26**

**The Big Picture**
- **US–Iran conflict** disrupting Middle East energy & chemicals
- Near-term **margin pressure** across pipes, tiles & wood panels
- Broad-based **price hikes now inevitable**

**PVC & Plastic Pipes**
- PVC resin up **47% since Jan-26** → **₹101/kg**
- Crude up **43%** to **$86.7/bbl**
- Some manufacturers **stopped taking orders** due to price volatility
- **Q4FY26** expected to be strongest quarter on volumes & EBITDA/tonne
- Inventory losses of **9MFY26 reversing into gains**

**Jal Jeevan Mission — Residual Demand**
- JJM outlay: **₹8.7 Tn** | Rural tap coverage: **17% → 82%**
- High pending states: **Rajasthan (41%)**, **WB (43%)**, **Jharkhand (45%)**, **Kerala (45%)**, **MP (26%)**
- Residual JJM work offers **further demand upside** for pipe players

**Ceramics — Severe Stress**
- Gujarat Gas cut Morbi supply to **50%** from March 6
- **~100 Morbi units** already shut
- Up to **70% factories** could shut if propane stops
- **~1,500 export containers** stuck due to Hormuz tensions
- War-risk surcharge: **$4,000–5,000/container** vs normal **$300–600**
- Profit/container **~$300** — margins completely **wiped out**

**Wood Panels — Cost Inflation**
- Methanol **+46%** | Formaldehyde **+35%** | Melamine **+25%**
- Technical Urea **+24%** | Wax **+13%**
- MDF cost inflation: **7–10%** depending on grade
- Particle board RMs up **40–60%**
- Price hikes of **5–10%** already guided by smaller players

**Key Takeaway**
- **Pipes:** Near-term positive — volume & inventory gains in Q4FY26
- **Ceramics:** High risk — export margins destroyed, factory shutdowns likely
- **Wood Panels:** Price hikes inevitable — margin protection key monitorable
- Current PVC spike seen as **unsustainable** — correction likely ahead

US Gold Cover: Debt Reality Check  

US gold reserves (8,133.5 tons, world's largest) now back just 3% of federal debt**—near record low despite gold's all-time highs.  

**Historical Shift  
- 1980: ~18% coverage (6x current). Matching requires gold at $26,000/oz (+400%).  
- 1940s: >50% backing. Parity demands $75,000/oz (+1,340%).  

Debt Explosion  
Reserves static while debt balloons—exposes fiscal imbalance gold bugs have flagged for years.  

Market Signal  
Low ratio fuels gold as hedge narrative, especially with Trump-era deficits. Inflation/BRICS de-dollar talk adds tailwinds. Relevant for your commodity watch (Gravita exposure)?

JSW Steel to Develop Coking Coal Mine in Mozambique

JSW Steel announced plans to develop a coking coal mining project in Mozambique to secure long-term raw material supply for its steel operations.

The project, Minas de Revuboè (MdR), is located in the Moatize coal basin in Tete Province.

The mine holds ~850 million tonnes of coal reserves, with potential to produce about 250 million tonnes of usable coking coal.

JSW plans to develop the mine in phases, with the first phase expected to be completed in about 2.5 years.

Initial production target is 2.4 million tonnes per year of prime hard coking coal.

Multiple De-Rating — The Case of Hindustan Foods

The Core Problem
- Stock remains largely flat over last 5 years
- Despite strong financial performance by the company
- Root cause: Extremely high starting valuation

Valuation Journey
- 2021: Stock trading at 150x+ P/E
- Today: Multiples compressed to ~40x
- Sharp de-rating of 110x+ turns over 4 years

Where It Stands Now
- Management guided PAT of ₹200–220 Cr for FY27
- Stock currently trading at ~25–26x FY27E earnings
- Valuation has significantly normalised

The Irony
- Business execution has remained consistently strong
- Yet stock has delivered near-zero returns
- Investors who paid 150x are still sitting on losses

The Key Lesson
- Entry valuation matters more than business quality
- Even great businesses can be bad investments at wrong prices
- De-rating can silently erode returns for years

Broader Pattern
- This is not an isolated case
- Similar de-rating has played out across multiple stocks
- High P/E entry = long years of time correction ahead

**Why SIPs Thrive in Crashes: 2008 Proof**  

**Peak vs. Dip Reality**  
- **Jan 2008 Peak (NAV ₹16.84)**: ₹10K SIP → **79K** (XIRR 12.8%), **593 units**.  

**Power of Falling NAVs**  
- NAV crashes to **₹12, 9, 7, 6, 5** → SIP buys **~1,000 units** (more shares at bargain prices).  

**Dip Installments Win Big**  
- **2009 Bottom SIP**: ₹10K → **₹2.8L** (₹2.7L gain vs. ₹69K at peak).  
- **Similar holding periods**, but **exponential unit accumulation** drives superior returns.  

**Strategy Edge**  
- **Continue SIPs**: Rupee cost averaging maximizes long-term compounding.  
- **Legends top up**; **ultra-legends lump sum** on deep dips.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Falling markets = **SIP superpower**—buy more units cheap, ride the rebound for outsized gains like 2008’s 28x bottom returns. Stay invested.

**JEFFERIES ON BANKS: LIMITED BUYBACKS, DIVIDEND POTENTIAL AHEAD**

**Global Buyback Boom**  
- Global banks doubled buybacks to **>$260B in 2025**, driven by higher ROE, excess capital, and slow growth.

**Indian Banks' Parallel Setup**  
- Indian banks show **rising ROE** and **slowing growth** (still above global peers).  
- Jefferies sees **limited buyback scope** despite similar tailwinds.

**Dividend Cap Hike Unlocks Value**  
- Recent **hike in dividend payout cap** enables select banks to lift shareholder returns.

**High Capital, High Payout Candidates**  
- **HDFC Bank, Union Bank, Bank of India** lead with strong capital and payout flexibility.

**Capital-Rich but Cautious Players**  
- **Kotak Bank, CSB Bank** have high capitalization but have kept payouts low.

**Key Takeaway:**  
**Prioritize dividend upside in HDFC/PSUs over buybacks — ROE strength favors payout hikes in a capped repurchase environment.**

Jefferies On Banks

BROKERAGE RADAR

Evaluating Buyback Optionality Among Indian Banks

In 2025, buybacks by global banks doubled to >$260 billion aided by higher ROE, capital and slow growth

Indian banks' ROE has risen & growth has slowed, but is still above peers

So we see limited scope for buybacks, but with hike in cap on dividend, some can raise payout

HDFC, Union Bank, and Bank of India have higher capital and payout

Kotak Bank and CSB have higher capitalisation, but have kept payouts low
Iran 🇮🇷 has ~10× the population and far larger geography, yet Israel’s 🇮🇱 economy is bigger and its income per person is ~10× higher.

**India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap**

• **NBFCs share:** ~21% of total credit  
• **Banks share:** Majority of remaining credit

• **Core issue:** Not a capital shortage, but a **credit translation problem**

• **Credit gap:** MSMEs, microfinance & small-ticket borrowers face **₹25–30 trillion deficit**

• **Economic role:** These segments contribute **~30–40% of India’s GDP**

• **Employment:** Employ **majority of India’s non-farm workforce**
**Strategic mapping of top transformer players in India**

text:
**INDIA EV AUTO: LEGACY OEMs DOMINATE UPTREND**

**Big Picture**  
EV upcycle intact across E-2W/3W/PV, but **legacy OEMs consolidating** (~80% share) as startups falter on service/capital/execution. FY26 volumes: E-2W >1.3 Mn (+15%), E-PV ~200k (+84%), E-3W +16% YTD.

**E-2W Leadership**  
TVS **28%**, Bajaj **23%**, Ather **18%**, Hero **11%** = **80% share**.  
TVS iQube peaks ~35k/mo; Bajaj Chetak C2501 <₹1L; Ather Rizta family pivot; Hero VIDA BaaS ₹45k + ₹0.96/km.  
**Ola collapse**: ~53k → ~4k/mo on service woes.

**E-PV Concentration**  
Tata **40%**, JSW MG **25-30%**, Mahindra **15%** = **85%**.  
Tata full-stack (Tiago to Harrier.ev, BaaS Punch); Mahindra BE6/XEV 9e scaling to 7-8k pm FY27; JSW Windsor >75%, MG 520 PHEV Oct'26.  
Maruti e-Vitara entry; Hyundai/BYD ~3-4%.

**E-3W Acceleration**  
Fastest growth; fleet-driven (passenger/e-comm).  
Bajaj overtakes Mahindra; TVS +1400% YTD low-base. Subsidy end Dec'25 but momentum holds.

**Company Spotlights**  
**TVS**: iQube + Orbiter; ~33% EV growth vs 15% industry.  
**Bajaj**: Chetak/GoGo; 2W/3W swapping.  
**Mahindra**: Treo/e-Alfa ~12% 3W EV share FY26; XEV 9S deliveries.  
**Tata Motors**: Punch/Harrier/Curvv ramps.

**Themes**  
Service networks/reliability win; TCO > subsidies; GST ICE cuts narrow gap but EVs hold on running costs (~₹0.2/km vs ₹2 petrol). BaaS widens access.

**Key Takeaway**  
Legacy scale (TVS/Bajaj/Tata/Mahindra) trumps startups—position for consolidation phase.

Asia’s Hormuz Exposure Scorecard – Nomura

• Energy Security Risk: Asia is at the epicenter of potential energy supply shocks if disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz continue.

• Stagflation Risk: Prolonged disruption (>1 month) could trigger stagflation — higher inflation with slower growth.

• Most Exposed Economies:
  – Thailand
  – South Korea
  – India

• Key Reason: High dependence on Middle East oil and gas imports passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

Implication:  
Sustained disruption could lead to higher oil prices, rising inflation and slower economic growth across Asia.

- Nifty Auto down **12% YTD** vs Nifty **-9.5%**; some stocks back to pre-GST cut levels  

**Preferred Picks**  
- Favour **Maruti, M&M, Hyundai** for better growth visibility and valuation support  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Autos face production and margin headwinds, but leaders like Maruti/M&M/Hyundai offer relative safety in the drawdown.

**Rating & Target**  
- **Maintains BUY**  
- **Target Price**: ₹360  

**Strategic Focus**  
- Capacity expansion + logistics scale-up drives FY28 earnings inflection  
- Leveraging Navkar synergies for pan-India multimodal network  

**FY30 Ambition**  
- Target: **₹8,000 Cr revenue** at **25% EBITDA margin**.

**Growth Projections**  
- Volume CAGR: **13% FY25-28**  
- Revenue/EBITDA CAGR: **33%****/28****% FY25-28**  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Logistics transformation positions JSW Infra for earnings inflection and market dominance.

**MOSL: VA TECH WABAG BUY @ ₹1,900**

**Rating & Target**  
- **Maintains BUY**  
- **Target Price**: ₹1,900  

**Growth Positioning**  
- Sustained inflows + normal ME project execution despite tensions.

- Revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR: **17%****/22****%****/23****% FY25-28**.

- Improving return ratios  
- Attractive at **~18x/15x FY27/28E P/E**.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Order pipeline + FCF strength supports Wabag's multi-year earnings compound at rich multiples.

**ELARA POWER UTILITIES: FEB UPDATE**

**Capacity Boom**  
- FY26 YTD: **45GW added** (vs 33GW FY25, 25GW FY24, 16GW FY23)—strongest 4-year start  
- Breakdown: **Solar 35GW**, Wind 4.6GW, Thermal 6GW, Hydro 3.4GW  
- Peak demand hits **244GW**; IEX volume +30.4% YoY to **12,550 MU**  

**Generation Lag**  
- Feb: +1.6% YoY to **145 BU**  
- FY26 YTD: Mere +0.8% YoY to **1,734 BU**  

**Top Picks**  
- **NTPC, Power Grid**: Regulated assets + capacity pipeline  
- **CESC**: Renewables expansion upside  
- **NLC India**: Double regulated equity by FY30  
- **Long-term**: Hydro sector revival  

**Key Takeaway:**  
45GW addition favors regulated PSUs—position for NTPC/Power Grid stability with CESC/NLC growth.

text:
**ELARA: POWER UTILITIES PICKS**

**Top Regulated Bets**  
- **NTPC & Power Grid**: Assured returns from regulated assets + strong capacity pipeline  

**Growth Upside**  
- **CESC**: Renewables expansion targets drive potential  
- **NLC India**: Attractive plan to double regulated equity by FY30  

**Long-Term Theme**  
Hydro sector promising with upcoming capacity addition and industry focus.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Regulated PSUs offer stability—layer in CESC/NLC for renewables/hydro growth kicker.

**CLSA INDIA: EL NIÑO THEMATIC**

**Risk Profile**  
El Niño looms with heatwave demand surge.

**Rural/Monsoon Hit**  
- Below-normal rainfall stokes food inflation  
- Rural incomes pressured—**downside risk to tractor demand**.

**Demand Boosters**  
- **Heat-sensitive winners**: ACs, coolers, refrigerators, fans  
- **Summer FMCG**: Ice creams, beverages, dairy primed for uplift.

**Power/Coal Squeeze**  
Rising power demand + global coal prices tighten non-power supply, lift **Coal India e-auction premiums**.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Position for heatwave consumer plays while avoiding monsoon-vulnerable rural names.

**Next Decade Tech Revolution: 2026-2030 Timeline**

**2026: AI Agent Explosion**  
AI becomes co-worker for research, coding, support, marketing.  
**Hits**: Marketing, content, junior dev, legal research.  

**2027: Autonomous AI**  
AI runs full workflows: software builds, campaigns, logistics.  
Copilots everywhere (doctors, lawyers, engineers).  

**2028: Early AGI**  
AI matches human learning across domains (~50% chance).  
Productivity surges; smaller teams suffice.  

**2029: Quantum Leap**  
Breaks drug discovery, materials, crypto.  
Leaders: IBM, Google, IonQ.  

**2030: Job Shift**  
**30% work hours automated**: Service, data, admin, basic coding.  
**New roles**: AI ethics, robotics, bio engineers.  

**Growth Winners**  
- **AI**: OpenAI, Nvidia, Microsoft.  
- **Energy**: Tesla Energy, NextEra.  
- **Biotech**: Moderna, Recursion.  
- **Space/Robotics**: SpaceX, Boston Dynamics.  

**Key Takeaway**  
**Adaptability trumps all** — learn tools faster to thrive in AI/quantum/robotics wave.
**Who Depends Most On Hormuz Oil Route 🛢🚢**

**CITI: WEST ASIA RISKS FOR BANKS**

**Macro Headwinds**  
West Asia tensions add risks via higher crude, trade disruption, FX/liquidity volatility, wider CAD.

**Sector Positioning**  
Banks enter from strength: decade-low GNPAs, robust CET-1 ratios.

**Conflict Scenarios**  
Prolonged tensions pressure asset quality, liquidity, market risk, credit growth.

**Defensive Ranking**  
Private Banks > PSUs > NBFCs.

**Preferred Picks**  
**HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, RBL Bank**.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Strong balance sheets cushion banks, but favor top private names amid geopolitical storm.

**UBS INITIATES: EUREKA FORBES BUY**

**Rating & Target**  
- **CMP**: ₹445  
- **Initiate BUY**  
- **Target**: ₹640  

**Water Purifier Inflection**  
- 45% revenue segment at takeoff due to low penetration.

**Growth Drivers**  
- Capex, R&D, innovation to drive **150 bps share gain FY25-28**  
- Higher-margin service/filter biz expands margins **250 bps**  

**Category Tailwinds**  
- Vacuum cleaners & air purifiers at **18% CAGR FY26-30**.

**Financial Projections**  
- Revenue/EBITDA CAGR: **13%****/22****% FY25-28**.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Low penetration + margin levers position Eureka Forbes for multi-year re-rating.

**MOSL: RIL BUY @ ₹1,750**

**Rating & Target**  
- **Maintains BUY**  
- **Target Price**: ₹1,750  

**Geopolitical Tailwind**  
- Middle East chaos disrupts **20% global crude/product supply**  
- Strong refining environment lifts O2C earnings  

**Historical Precedent**  
Russia-Ukraine war showed elevated spreads persist long-term.

**Outlook**  
Even if tensions ease, supply chain lags keep product cracks high—supporting RIL's refining/petrochem margins.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Supply disruptions act as catalyst for RIL's O2C strength amid sustained high spreads.

**HSBC: COAL INDIA HOLD**

**Rating & Target**  
- **Recommendation**: HOLD  
- **Target Price**: ₹420 (up from ₹380)  

**Near-Term Positives**  
- Higher e-auction premiums benefit short term  
- Elevated gas/regional coal prices boost premiums and volumes  

**Structural Concerns**  
- Oversupply of domestic coal persists  
- Weak thermal demand caps upside  

**Earnings View**  
FY28 earnings unchanged—oversupply dynamics to pressure premiums lower.

**Stock Outlook**  
Near-term outperformance from Middle East tensions, but gains fade post gas supply normalization.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Tactical premium pop supports Coal India but structural oversupply limits re-rating.

**JEFFERIES: RELIANCE INDUSTRIES BUY**

**Rating & Target**  
- **Recommendation**: BUY  
- **Target Price**: ₹1,740 (down from ₹1,820)  

**Defensive Appeal**  
Stock offers stability amid current volatility.

**O2C Tailwinds**  
- Middle East disruptions boost refining/petrochem spreads  
- Expect elevated spreads to sustain through conflict  
- Higher margins projected for HiFY27  

**Jio Adjustments**  
Lowered FY27/28E EBITDA by 10%/6% due to delayed tariff hikes to Dec-26.

**Earnings Outlook**  
Raised consolidated FY27 EBITDA by 2% on O2C strength.

**Valuation**  
Trades below long-term average—limited downside with earnings support.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Reliance emerges as defensive pick with O2C windfall offsetting Jio delays.

**BROKERAGE HIGHLIGHTS**

**Macquarie – Auto Sector Caution**  
- Macro uncertainty fuels growth risks  
- March-26 retail/production held up—until now  
- Earnings risks from demand weakness, margin squeeze  
- **Outlook**: India autos to underperform Nifty near-term  

**Jefferies – Top Picks March 2026**  
- **23 BUYs vs 8 Underperforms**  
- **New BUYs**: SBI, Star Health, Groww, Bharat Forge, JSW Steel, Eternal, Max Healthcare  
- **New Underperforms**: Hyundai, Cipla, Wipro  
- **Upgrades**: Delhivery → BUY; Laurus → HOLD  

**Morgan Stanley – Real Estate Data Boom**  
- Global AI capex to lift India data centers to **10.5GW by FY31**  
- **Highest exposure**: Lodha, Mindspace  
- **Others**: DLF, Prestige  
- Developers edge: land access, approvals, construction expertise  
- Data centers offer faster growth + premium valuations  
- **Lodha/Mindspace**: >20% upside from DC expansion alone  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Rotate from autos to Jefferies BUYs and data center leaders amid sector rotation plays.

**BOFA: CPI INFLATION REVIEW**

**Brokerage Radar**  
February CPI shows inflation rising but more gradually.

**CPI Breakdown**  
- Food prices higher, services remain in check  
- Inflation in control despite West Asia conflict uncertainty  

**Conflict Impact**  
- Ongoing conflict likely hits WPI prices in March print  
- CPI impact limited by government intervention managing pump prices  

**Energy Sensitivity**  
Every $10/barrel increase in energy prices lifts headline inflation by 25-30 bps per year.

**RBI Outlook**  
RBI likely holds rates for extended period with neutral stance.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Gradual CPI rise plus govt fuel intervention supports prolonged RBI rate pause.

**BROKERAGE RADAR: EL NIÑO THREAT**

**Weather Outlook**  
El Niño risk looms over monsoon season.

**Sector Pressures**  
- Heatwave demand surges, monsoon-linked sectors brace for impact  
- Hotter temperatures and below-normal rainfall stoke food inflation  
- Rural incomes pressured, posing downside risk for tractor demand  

**Demand Winners**  
- Heat-sensitive categories primed: ACs, coolers, refrigerators, fans  
- Summer FMCG boosted: ice creams, beverages, dairy  

**Coal Squeeze**  
Rising power demand and higher global coal prices tighten non-power availability, pushing up Coal India e-auction prices.

**Key Takeaway:**  
El Niño tilts playbook toward heatwave winners while rural/monsoon plays face downside risks.

**UBS: CPI INFLATION ALERT**

**Brokerage Radar**  
February CPI inflation hit **3.2% YoY**, beating consensus expectations.

**Key Pressure Points**  
- Rising energy prices fueling near-term inflation  
- Iran shock has markets pricing RBI rate hike in 2026  
- Food inflation continues accelerating  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Hotter-than-expected CPI plus energy/food pressures signal tighter RBI policy ahead.
Via: X

**MACQUARIE: AUTO SECTOR CAUTION**

**Brokerage Radar**  
Macro uncertainty lends to growth risk.

**Recent Performance**  
March-26 retail and production have held up well, until now.

**Earnings Risks**  
Focus now on risks from demand weakness and margin pressures.

**Prior View**  
Remained constructive expecting healthy demand and steady/improving margins.

**Updated Outlook**  
Growth uncertainty plus margin risks mean India auto stocks likely to underperform broad markets near term.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Auto sector vulnerable to demand-margin pressures—underperformance likely ahead.

**MACQUARIE: AUTO SECTOR CAUTION**

**Brokerage Radar**  
Macro uncertainty heightens growth risks for auto sector.

**Recent Strength**  
March-26 retail and production held up well—until now.

**Shift in Focus**  
Earnings risks now center on weakening demand and margin pressures.

**Previous Stance**  
Remained constructive expecting healthy demand and steady/improving margins.

**Updated Outlook**  
Growth uncertainty plus margin risks point to India auto stocks underperforming broader markets near term.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Auto sector faces demand-margin double whammy—defensive positioning warranted.

**JEFFERIES INDIA STRATEGY: TOP IDEAS MARCH 2026**

**Brokerage Radar**  
Bottom-up analyst top ideas for March 2026 feature **23 BUYs** and **8 Underperforms**.

**Fresh BUY Additions**  
- **SBI, Star Health, Groww**  
- **Bharat Forge, JSW Steel**  
- **Eternal, Max Healthcare**

**New Underperform Calls**  
- **Hyundai, Cipla, Wipro**

**Recent Upgrades**  
- **Delhivery** upgraded from Underperform to **BUY**  
- **Laurus** upgraded from Underperform to **HOLD**

**Key Takeaway:**  
Jefferies tilts bullish with 23 BUYs vs 8 Underperforms—watch fresh banking, health, and metals conviction plays.

**MORGAN STANLEY: REAL ESTATE DATA CENTER BOOM**

**Brokerage Radar**  
Global AI capex explosion could boost India's data center capacity to **10.5GW by FY31**.

**Top Exposure Plays**  
- **Lodha & Mindspace** lead with highest data center exposure  
- **DLF & Prestige** carry meaningful exposure  

**Developer Advantages**  
- Access to prime land parcels  
- Expertise in navigating approvals  
- Proven experience building structures  
- Track record housing rental assets  

**Why Data Centers Win**  
Returns match commercial assets but offer faster development, superior rental growth, and valuation premiums.

**Upside Potential**  
**Lodha & Mindspace** could deliver **>20% upside** from current levels via data center expansion alone.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Land-rich developers positioned for data center re-rating as AI drives capacity explosion.

**Equirus Bullish on Lenskart: LONG ₹625 TP**

**Coverage Initiation**  
- **LONG rating** with **₹625 target price**.  
- India's **underpenetrated eyewear market** formalizing rapidly.  

**Competitive Edge**  
- Lenskart positioned via **manufacturing scale**, **centralized supply chain**, **omnichannel distribution**.  
- Mirrors global leaders like **EssilorLuxottica** and **Specsavers** for share gains.  

**Growth Projections**  
- **Market share**: **5% → 8%** by FY30E.  
- **Store CAGR**: **~16%**.  
- **Revenue CAGR (FY25–28E)**: **~24%**.  

**Margin Outlook**  
- **Operating leverage** to drive significant margin expansion.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Equirus sees Lenskart capturing eyewear market formalization via scale advantages, targeting 24% revenue growth and 8% share by FY30.
